enonce.texte: Complete the following conditional sentences with the correct form of the verbs in parentheses.

questions.texte: If you ________ (heat) water to 100°C, it ________ (boil).
questions.reponse: heat / boils

questions.texte: If she ________ (study) hard, she ________ (pass) the exam.
questions.reponse: studies / will pass

questions.texte: If it ________ (rain) tomorrow, we ________ (not go) to the market.
questions.reponse: rains / will not go

questions.texte: If you ________ (mix) red and blue, you ________ (get) purple.
questions.reponse: mix / get

questions.texte: If I ________ (have) enough money, I ________ (buy) a new phone.
questions.reponse: have / will buy

enonce.texte: Complete the following conditional sentences with the correct form of the verbs in parentheses.

questions.texte: If I ________ (be) a doctor, I ________ (help) people in rural areas.
questions.reponse: were / would help

questions.texte: If she ________ (study) harder last year, she ________ (pass) the GCE.
questions.reponse: had studied / would have passed

questions.texte: If we ________ (live) in Douala, we ________ (visit) the beach more often.
questions.reponse: lived / would visit

questions.texte: If they ________ (arrive) early, they ________ (not miss) the ceremony.
questions.reponse: had arrived / would not have missed

questions.texte: If I ________ (know) his address, I ________ (send) him a letter.
questions.reponse: knew / would send

enonce.texte: Read each sentence and identify the type of conditional used (Type 0, Type 1, Type 2, or Type 3). Then explain your choice.

questions.texte: If you heat ice, it melts.
questions.reponse: Type 0 (general truth)

sousQuestions.reponse: This is a type 0 conditional because it expresses a general scientific fact. Both clauses are in the present simple tense.

questions.texte: If it rains tomorrow, I will stay home.
questions.reponse: Type 1 (real possibility)

sousQuestions.reponse: This is a type 1 conditional because it expresses a real and possible situation in the future. The if-clause uses present simple, and the main clause uses will + base verb.

questions.texte: If I were rich, I would travel the world.
questions.reponse: Type 2 (hypothetical present/future)

sousQuestions.reponse: This is a type 2 conditional because it expresses an imaginary or hypothetical situation in the present. The if-clause uses past simple (were), and the main clause uses would + base verb.

questions.texte: If they had left earlier, they would have caught the bus.
questions.reponse: Type 3 (unreal past)

sousQuestions.reponse: This is a type 3 conditional because it expresses an unreal situation in the past. The if-clause uses past perfect (had left), and the main clause uses would have + past participle.

enonce.texte: Read the following scenarios and complete the conditional sentences based on the context.

questions.texte: You are in Cameroon during the rainy season. What usually happens if it rains heavily in the morning?
questions.reponse: If it rains heavily in the morning, the roads often get flooded.

questions.texte: You are advising a friend who wants to pass the GCE exam. What should they do?
questions.reponse: If you want to pass the GCE exam, you should study hard and practice past questions.

questions.texte: You are thinking about volunteering in your community. What would you do if you were asked to help?
questions.reponse: If I were asked to help, I would gladly volunteer my time and skills.

questions.texte: You are talking about a past event. Your friend missed an important meeting because he didn't wake up on time. What would have happened if he had set his alarm?
questions.reponse: If he had set his alarm, he wouldn't have missed the meeting.

enonce.texte:
Read the text about Cameroon and answer the questions using conditional sentences.

"In Cameroon, many young people want to contribute to the development of their communities. However, unemployment remains a major challenge. The government has implemented various programs to encourage youth entrepreneurship. If young people receive proper training, they can create jobs. Many experts believe that if the government invested more in education, the country would experience faster economic growth. Unfortunately, some opportunities were missed in the past. If the government had built more infrastructure, the country would have improved its international competitiveness."

questions.texte: What can happen if young people receive proper training according to the text?
questions.reponse: If young people receive proper training, they can create jobs.

questions.texte: What would happen if the government invested more in education?
questions.reponse: If the government invested more in education, the country would experience faster economic growth.

questions.texte: What would have happened if the government had built more infrastructure?
questions.reponse: If the government had built more infrastructure, the country would have improved its international competitiveness.

intitule: Using Conditionals to Express Opinions and Advise

enonce.texte: Read the following situations and write appropriate conditional sentences to give advice or express opinions.

questions.texte: Your friend wants to start a small business in Cameroon but doesn't have much money. Give him advice using type 1 conditional.
questions.reponse: If you want to start a business, you should start small and look for government grants.

questions.texte: You are talking about the problem of drug abuse among young people in Cameroon. Express an opinion on what should be done using type 1 or type 2 conditional.
questions.reponse: If we want to reduce drug abuse, we should invest more in education and awareness campaigns. / If we taught young people about the dangers of drugs, they would be less likely to try them.

questions.texte: You are talking about a past election. Some people didn't vote because they thought their voice didn't matter. What would have happened if more people had voted?
questions.reponse: If more people had voted, the election results might have been different.

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following conditional sentences using inversion (e.g., Were I you... / Had I known...).

questions.texte: If I were you, I would accept the offer.
questions.reponse: Were I you, I would accept the offer.

questions.texte: If he had studied harder, he would have passed the exam.
questions.reponse: Had he studied harder, he would have passed the exam.

questions.texte: If she knew the answer, she would tell us.
questions.reponse: Were she to know the answer, she would tell us. / Did she know the answer, she would tell us.

questions.texte: If I had known about the meeting, I would have attended.
questions.reponse: Had I known about the meeting, I would have attended.
